notes.type_correctness: THEME_IDS tuple, ThemeId union, THEMES record, and THEME_ID_ALLOWLIST are all consistent. TypeScript build passes with no new errors. XtermTheme interface enforces all 21 required fields at compile time.
notes.pattern_adherence: Implementation follows the established theme-extension pattern exactly: PALETTE const → ThemeDescriptor const → THEMES registry entry → CSS block → server allowlist. Comment separators, field ordering, and naming conventions match all other themes.
notes.test_coverage: 4 theme-related tests needed updating due to the new theme count and DEFAULT_THEME change; all fixed and passing. 2 pre-existing GlobalSettingsPage webhook failures (unrelated to this change) and 1 pre-existing flaky benchmark test remain — confirmed pre-existing via git stash check.
notes.security: Server allowlist correctly gates PATCH /api/hub/theme. No new API surface. No user-supplied data flows through the theme CSS block. Bogus-value 400 path is unchanged.
notes.architectural_alignment: Change is purely additive — no existing code mutated beyond extending the two data structures and changing one constant. DEFAULT_THEME change is intentional per spec. CSS block respects the semantic-token contract with no brand-named (dracula-*) tokens.
